seoTitle: Set Up Email Automations in Wix Forms
metaDescription: Learn how to trigger automatic email responses when someone submits a form on your Wix website, including personalization.
intro: Instead of manually replying to every form submission, Wix lets you build automated email responses triggered by form activity. This guide covers setting up an automation from your form trigger, designing the email content, and personalizing messages with dynamic tokens like the sender's name.

steps.heading: Open the Automations panel
steps.body: From your Wix dashboard, navigate to the Automations control panel.

steps.heading: Create a new automation
steps.body: Click New Automation and select your target form submission as the trigger.

steps.heading: Choose the Send an email action
steps.body: Set the response action to send an email whenever the trigger fires.

steps.heading: Design your email
steps.body: Use the email designer to set colors, add your logo, and write the message body.

steps.heading: Personalize with tokens
steps.body: Insert dynamic tokens like the sender's first name to make each email feel personal rather than generic.

steps.heading: Set timing and test
steps.body: Choose whether the email sends instantly or after a delay, then submit a test entry to confirm delivery.

faq.question: Can I delay an automated email instead of sending it instantly?
faq.answer: Yes, the automation timing settings let you apply a strategic delay rather than sending the moment a form is submitted.

faq.question: Can I personalize the email with the submitter's name?
faq.answer: Yes, personalization tokens like the sender's first name can be inserted into the email body for a more tailored message.

faq.question: Do I need a specific form to trigger this automation?
faq.answer: Yes, you'll select the specific form submission event that acts as the trigger when setting up the automation.

faq.question: Where can I check if automated emails are sending correctly?
faq.answer: Review the automation activity log panel to confirm complete transaction tracking after your live tests.
